creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to andrew from comment #2)

I do not see how what I wrote contradicts your original
or new material.

I'd guess that FreeBSD will at some point choose to support
VFP use and NEON use in some form for processors that have
them.

But for now the supported context requires that libthr (or its
signal handling) be built without use of such. Similarly for
any other signal-handling code.

The notes about the required ABI change were a good thing to
be explicit about as well. stable/11 may never get such since
after release ABI changes are avoided. Fixing 11 by changing
its ABI likely would require a general debate before potential
approval.

Thus stable/11 may literally never officially allow builds
where libthr uses NEON in its signal handlers, for example.
Nor openssl using NEON for another example.

head (12) likely will eventually support such, or that would
be my guess.
